metadata.description: Complete class structure for all major Indian and international school boards from Pre-Primary to Class 12

CBSE.governed_by: School level — CBSE does not regulate pre-primary

ICSE.governed_by: School level — CISCE does not regulate pre-primary

State_Boards.note: Varies by state; NEP 2020 formalises as Balvatika 1

grades.curriculum_focus: Play-based learning, motor skills, language exposure, social skills
Early literacy, early numeracy, foundational language skills
Readiness for formal schooling, pre-writing, early comprehension

grades.key_notes: Foundation literacy and numeracy. NEP 2020 calls this part of the 5+3+3+4 structure's 'Foundational Stage' (age 3-8).
Continuation of foundational stage. Reading fluency and basic arithmetic consolidation.
NEP 2020 'Preparatory Stage' begins at Class 3 (age 8-11). Introduction of formal subject boundaries in some boards.
Science and Social Studies begin separating from EVS in ICSE and most state boards at this level.

IB_PYP.called: PYP Year 8 (Final PYP year — PYP Exhibition)

board_transitions.IB: PYP ends at Grade 5. Students move to MYP from Grade 6.
board_transitions.IGCSE: Cambridge Primary ends. Students move to Cambridge Lower Secondary (Grade 6).
board_transitions.CBSE: Primary stage ends. Middle stage begins at Class 6.
board_transitions.State_Boards: Some states have board exams / scholarship exams at Class 5 (e.g. NTS, state talent search).

grades.key_notes: Primary stage completion for CBSE. IB PYP Exhibition — major transdisciplinary project.
NEP 2020 Middle Stage: Class 6-8. Science bifurcates into Physics, Chemistry, Biology in ICSE from Class 6.
Algebra, geometry, and essay writing become core. NTSE, Olympiads preparation begins in many schools.

State_Boards.note: Some states have Class 8 board exams

board_transitions.IGCSE: Cambridge Lower Secondary Checkpoint exams taken at end of Year 9 (Class 8 equivalent). Students then move to IGCSE (Grade 9-10).
board_transitions.CBSE: Middle stage ends; Secondary (9-10) begins next year.

grades.key_notes: NEP 2020 Middle Stage ends at Class 8. Some state boards (UP, Rajasthan) hold formal Class 8 board exams.

CBSE.note: SA1/SA2 internal exams; CBSE board begins at Class 10

State_Boards.note: Preparatory year for Class 10 board in most states

grades.key_notes: NEP 2020 Secondary Stage: Class 9-12. IGCSE formally begins at Class 9. IB MYP Personal Project begins.

ICSE.board_exam: ICSE Examination (Indian Certificate of Secondary Education)

IB_MYP.note: Some schools do not offer MYP eAssessment and transition to IGCSE or Diploma

WBBSE.governing_body: West Bengal Board of Secondary Education

compulsory: Hindi A or Hindi B or Sanskrit or Urdu
CBSE.note: Students choose 5 main subjects + 1 optional (6th subject)

ICSE.note: ICSE has 3 groups; English compulsory; 2 subjects from each of Group 2 & 3 required

IB_MYP.personal_project: Independent MYP Personal Project completed in Year 5

IGCSE.note: Students typically choose 7-10 IGCSE subjects. Minimum 5 required for most pathways. Core vs Extended tiers available per subject.

State_Boards_General.note: Subject names and groupings vary by state; generally 5-6 subjects for the board exam

grades.key_notes: Most critical board exam year in Indian schooling. Class 10 marks are required for school admissions, scholarships, and stream selection for Class 11-12.

higher_secondary.bifurcation_note: Class 11 is the major bifurcation point in Indian education. Students choose a stream — Science, Commerce, or Arts/Humanities — that determines subject choices for both Class 11 and Class 12. This choice also determines eligibility for most professional entrance exams (JEE/NEET for Science; CA/CS/CMA for Commerce; CLAT/CUET-Arts for Humanities).

Science.description: Leads to JEE (Engineering), NEET (Medical), NDA, and most tech/research careers

PCMB.name: Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology
PCMB.note: 5th subject combination; attempted by students targeting both JEE and NEET

Commerce.description: Leads to CA, CS, CMA, BBA, BCom, Economics, Finance careers

Arts_Humanities.description: Leads to Law, Journalism, Civil Services, Teaching, Design, Social Sciences

Vocational.description: Skill-based streams introduced under NEP 2020 and NSQF
Vocational.note: Available in CBSE (Vocational stream), State Boards (ITI/Polytechnic prep); NSQF Level 4 certification

CBSE.exam_type: School-based internal exam; no CBSE board exam at Class 11
CBSE.stream_selection: At the time of Class 11 admission after Class 10 results

ICSE_ISC.note: ISC (Indian School Certificate) is the Class 11-12 programme under CISCE; equivalent to CBSE Class 11-12

IGCSE_AS.called: AS Level Year 1 or Cambridge International AS & A Level Year 1
IGCSE_AS.note: After IGCSE, students may move to A Levels (Grade 11-12)

KSEEB.note: Karnataka uses PUC instead of HSC; governing body is Dept of Pre-University Education

WBCHSE.governing_body: West Bengal Council of Higher Secondary Education

BIEAP.governing_body: Board of Intermediate Education Andhra Pradesh

BIETELANGANA.governing_body: Telangana State Board of Intermediate Education

grades.key_notes: Stream/subject selection at Class 11 is the most consequential academic decision in Indian schooling. It directly determines entrance exam eligibility.

A_LEVEL.credential: Cambridge International AS & A Level Certificate

IB_DP.structure: 6 subjects across 6 groups (3 at Higher Level, 3 at Standard Level) + Theory of Knowledge (TOK) + Extended Essay + CAS
IB_DP.credential_requirements: Minimum 24 points (out of 45) to receive IB Diploma; 7 points per subject + up to 3 bonus points for TOK+EE

A_LEVEL.AS_note: AS Levels taken in Year 1 (Class 11); A Levels completed in Year 2 (Class 12)

CBSE.grading: Marks out of 100 per subject; CGPA optional

ISC.grading: Percentage-based; ISC known for rigorous English and Science standards

IB_DP.evaluation: Internal Assessment (IA) + External Examinations; TOK Essay + EE externally moderated
IB_DP.grading: Scale 1-7 per subject; Total 45 points max including TOK+EE bonus

A_LEVEL.evaluation: Primarily written exams; coursework component in some subjects
A_LEVEL.grading: A*-E grades per subject; UCAS tariff points for UK university applications

grades.key_notes: Class 12 board marks are used for CUET (Common University Entrance Test) eligibility, NDA eligibility, direct college admissions, and as a cut-off for many entrance exams. IB Diploma is internationally recognised and accepted by universities worldwide.

CBSE.governing_body: Ministry of Education, Government of India
key_features: NCERT textbooks used across the board
NEP 2020 being integrated — 5+3+3+4 structure
No detention policy till Class 8 (recently revised under NEP)
Competency-based assessment introduced from 2020
CUET (Common University Entrance Test) from 2022 for central university admissions
Two levels of Maths at Class 10: Standard (for science/commerce) and Basic (for arts/non-maths streams)

ICSE_ISC.full_name: Indian Certificate of Secondary Education / Indian School Certificate
ICSE_ISC.governing_body: Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE)

primary.note: School-based; CISCE regulates from Class 6 onwards in most schools

key_features: Known for rigorous English language education
Science taught as separate subjects (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) from Class 6
Project-based and practical-heavy assessment
Widely accepted for international university applications
Internal assessment carries significant weight (20-30%)
ISC (Class 11-12) has broad subject choices including humanities and arts

PYP.name: Primary Years Programme
PYP.approach: Transdisciplinary inquiry-based learning; 6 subject areas + Units of Inquiry

MYP.name: Middle Years Programme
MYP.approach: 8 subject groups + Personal Project in Year 5 (Grade 10); interdisciplinary

DP.name: Diploma Programme
DP.approach: 6 subjects (3 HL + 3 SL) + TOK + Extended Essay + CAS; internationally recognized

CP.name: Career-related Programme
CP.approach: Combines DP courses with career-related studies; for vocationally-oriented students

key_features: Holistic, inquiry-based international curriculum
IB Diploma accepted by 3,500+ universities in 150+ countries
Theory of Knowledge (TOK) — unique cross-curricular philosophy/epistemology course
Extended Essay — 4,000-word independent research paper
CAS (Creativity, Activity, Service) — mandatory co-curricular component
Internal Assessment + External Exams; 1-7 grading scale per subject
Available in English, French, and Spanish
Schools must be IB-authorized (accredited); ~200+ IB schools in India

IGCSE_A_LEVEL.full_name: International General Certificate of Secondary Education / Cambridge International AS & A Levels
IGCSE_A_LEVEL.governing_body: Cambridge Assessment International Education (CAIE), University of Cambridge

Cambridge_Primary.name: Cambridge Primary
Cambridge_Primary.subjects: English, Mathematics, Science (core); schools may add others

Cambridge_Lower_Secondary.name: Cambridge Lower Secondary
Cambridge_Lower_Secondary.checkpoint_exam: Cambridge Lower Secondary Checkpoint at end of Grade 9

IGCSE.name: Cambridge IGCSE
IGCSE.approach: Students choose 5-10 subjects; Core and Extended tiers per subject

AS_Level.name: Cambridge International AS Level
AS_Level.approach: Half of A Level; can be standalone credential

A_Level.name: Cambridge International A Level
A_Level.approach: 3-4 subjects at depth; IA + External exams

key_features: International curriculum recognised by 160+ countries
Rigorous assessment with dual-tier (Core/Extended) options in IGCSE
A Levels accepted for admission to UK, US, Australia, Canada, and Indian universities (CUET exemption for some)
Wide subject choice (50+ IGCSE subjects, 55+ A Level subjects)
Available in English only (primarily)
~500+ Cambridge schools in India

GSEB.full_name: Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board

streams: Science (A Group: PCM / B Group: PCB)

key_features: SSC exam at Std. 10; HSC at Std. 12
Gujarati as primary medium; English medium schools increasing
Vocational stream available at HSC
GSEB GUJCET for engineering/pharmacy admissions in Gujarat

MSBSHSE.full_name: Maharashtra State Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education

secondary.divisions: Pune, Mumbai, Nagpur, Aurangabad, Amravati, Konkan, Nashik, Latur, Kolhapur, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

key_features: Largest state board by student enrollment in India
SSC at Std. 10; HSC at Std. 12 via Junior Colleges
FYJC (First Year JC) and SYJC (Second Year JC) terminology unique to Maharashtra
MHT-CET used for engineering/pharmacy admission in Maharashtra
Marathi compulsory subject for Maharashtra students

TNBSE.full_name: Tamil Nadu Board of Secondary Education (also: Tamil Nadu State Board)
key_features: SSLC at Std. 10 is widely used for government job eligibility
100% marks culture in Tamil Nadu HSC — very high-scoring board
Tamil compulsory up to Class 12
TNEA (Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions) based on Class 12 marks without separate CET (recently changed)
Samacheer Kalvi (Equitable Education) uniform curriculum from 2010

KSEEB.medium_of_instruction: Kannada, English, Urdu, Telugu, Tamil, Marathi, Hindi

pre_university.note: Karnataka uses PUC (Pre-University Course) instead of HSC; governed by the Dept. of Pre-University Education, NOT KSEEB

key_features: Unique PUC system (Pre-University Course) for Grades 11-12
KCET (Karnataka Common Entrance Test) for professional course admissions
Kannada compulsory subject
KSEEB governs only up to Class 10; PUE Board governs 11-12

WBBSE_WBCHSE.full_name: West Bengal Board of Secondary Education (WBBSE) / West Bengal Council of Higher Secondary Education (WBCHSE)
WBBSE_WBCHSE.medium_of_instruction: Bengali, English, Hindi, Urdu, Nepali, Santali, Odia, Punjabi
key_features: Madhyamik (Class 10) is a prestigious and widely-taken board exam in Bengal
Separate boards for Secondary (WBBSE) and Higher Secondary (WBCHSE)
Bengali compulsory for Bengali-medium students
WBJEE for engineering admissions in West Bengal
Strong tradition of academic excellence in science and arts
One of the largest board exams by enrollment in India
Result controversies led to major reforms post-2016
Hindi medium dominant; English medium growing
BCECE for engineering/medical admissions in Bihar

MPBSE.full_name: Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education
key_features: MP Board is Hindi-medium dominant
One of the oldest state boards
Ruk Jana Nahi scheme for failed students to reappear
MP PET (Pre-Engineering Test) for state engineering colleges
Class 8 board exam is still conducted in Rajasthan
Hindi as primary medium
RPET for Rajasthan engineering admissions
Strong preparation base for IAS/UPSC aspirants
Punjabi compulsory for all students
Agriculture stream unique to Punjab
PMET and JEE for higher education in Punjab

BIEAP.full_name: Board of Intermediate Education Andhra Pradesh
key_features: Intermediate Board (BIEAP) separate from SSC Board (BSE AP) — two different bodies for 9-10 vs 11-12
AP EAMCET for engineering and medical admissions
Telugu compulsory; Telugu Medium dominant
Bifurcated from Telangana in 2014

BIETELANGANA.full_name: Telangana State Board of Intermediate Education
key_features: Created post-bifurcation from AP in 2014
TS EAMCET for professional course admissions
Strong focus on NEET/JEE coaching culture (Hyderabad coaching hub)
Telugu medium dominant; English medium growing

CHSE_ODISHA.full_name: Council of Higher Secondary Education, Odisha

secondary.governing_body: Board of Secondary Education, Odisha (BSE Odisha)

key_features: BSE Odisha handles Class 10; CHSE handles Class 11-12 (two separate bodies)
Odia compulsory
OJEE (Odisha Joint Entrance Examination) for engineering/MBA
Formed after Chhattisgarh was carved out of MP in 2000
Hindi medium dominant
CGPET for engineering admissions in CG

JKBOSE.full_name: Jammu and Kashmir Board of School Education
key_features: Two separate exam schedules: Jammu Division (Nov-Dec) and Kashmir Division (Mar-Apr) due to climate differences
Converted to UT in 2019; board remains JKBOSE
Urdu is the first language for most students in Kashmir division

UPMSP.full_name: Uttar Pradesh Madhyamik Shiksha Parishad (UP Board)
key_features: Largest single state board in the world by student enrollment (~55 lakh students appear each year)
Hindi medium dominant; many students shift to CBSE for better opportunities
Class 8 board exam recently discontinued
UP CPAT and JEE for admissions in UP
Hindi and English bilingual board
HSTES (Haryana State Technical Education Society) for polytechnic admissions
Strong NDA/Military career tradition in Haryana — PE and physical subjects emphasized

grade_equivalence_table.CBSE_ICSE_StateBoards: Class 11 (HSC Year 1 / FYJC / +1 / PUC 1 / Intermediate Year 1)

grade_equivalence_table.Cambridge: A Level Year 2 / A Level Certificate
